### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 2 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Build the Brief

### Build the Brief

Shapes the item: writes `brief`, `tone`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Draft with AI Writer

### Draft with AI Writer

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Next: Refine with AI Editor

### OpenAI Writer Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `gpt-4o-mini`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Draft with AI Writer** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenAI API key

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Next: Stage YouTube Description

### OpenAI Editor Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `gpt-4o-mini`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Publish short videos with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive in real time for Editing

**Pattern:** Video Production · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Publish short videos with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive in real time for Editing. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual editing is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Build the Brief → Draft with AI Writer → Refine with AI Editor → Stage YouTube Description → Align Field Names → Send Summary to Slack

## Use it for
- Teams that want to video production without manual effort
- Anyone automating editing in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Scales without extra headcount
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; OpenAI API key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your editing process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
